notes: Dialogic cited 2+ million installed ports; 2,000+ application partners in 74 countries.

metric_value: 7x24 worldwide; 2-hour response time
notes: CGI provided 7x24 service with guaranteed 2-hour remote response time.

metric_name: Kenan Arbor/BP billing speed on Alpha vs other platforms
metric_value: 50% faster on Alpha
notes: Kenan completed Alpha port December 1996; competitive benchmarking showed 50% faster billing processing rates vs other platforms.

metric_value: Billing rate processing not possible with 32-bit Intel
notes: Per Kenan: 'same telephone call processing and rating speed cannot be done with a 32-bit Intel platform.'

metric_value: Proactive fault detection before customer visibility
notes: TeMiPs could issue automatic network alerts and identify faults before they became visible to customers.

metric_value: European market leader for messaging
notes: CMG described as European market leader for short message service center systems. US market entry 1995 via Ericsson partnership.

metric_value: Software supports multiple gateways; open APIs; TAPI
notes: Vienna's IP voice/fax gateway supported multiple gateways, open APIs, TAPI applications; web-based support.

metric_value: Unified multi-service billing from single mechanism
notes: Arbor/BP allowed multiple telecom services customer to receive single bill through centralized data filtering.

metric_value: Partner-led ecosystem; systems integration focus
notes: DIGITAL's strategy: use partners to expand options, shorten learning curves, leverage systems integration strength.

metric_value: Intelligent Network, TMN, CC&B, Broadband Services
notes: DIGITAL targeted four telecom segments: IN, TMN, Customer Care & Billing, Broadband Services.

metric_value: Start-up; relied on DIGITAL brand for credibility
notes: Vienna was a small start-up for which DIGITAL partnership added prestige and credibility.

metric_value: Supporting US customers; expanding via partnerships; Ericsson network certification
notes: CMG entered US in 1995 via Ericsson partnership; seeking Ericsson certification to embed in network.

metric_value: Computing, communications, new media convergence
notes: Aberdeen identified convergence of computing, communications, and new media as key driver of telecom IT demand.

metric_value: Slow due to legacy analog incompatibility
notes: PCS 1900 adoption slow because many customers still relied on legacy analog technology incompatible with new standards.

metric_value: Increasing use in wireless/cellular for roaming and phone personality
notes: Smart cards driving remote wireless market: prepaid cards, roaming, phone personalities.

metric_name: Aberdeen conclusion on DIGITAL telecom positioning
metric_value: Well-positioned with best-in-class partners
notes: Aberdeen concluded DIGITAL well-positioned to expand in telecom with best-in-class partners and integration capabilities.

metric_value: Will expand presence in key telco segments
notes: Aberdeen predicted DIGITAL would expand telecom presence through its partner ecosystem and Alpha platform.

metric_value: Vienna products to ship to DIGITAL customers Spring 1997
notes: Vienna set Spring 1997 as target date for shipping to DIGITAL customers.

metric_value: CMG service platform soon to be certified by Ericsson
notes: CMG's platform expected to be certified by Ericsson and become embedded part of Ericsson's network.

metric_value: Alpha will differentiate DIGITAL from 32-bit competitors
notes: Aberdeen predicted Alpha's 64-bit performance advantage would sustain DIGITAL's telco competitive position.
